steps.title: Recognize that both equations represent the same line
steps.content: Notice that $10x + 15y = 35$ is exactly 5 times $2x + 3y = 7$. Since these are scalar multiples, they represent the same line. The system has infinitely many solutions: every point on that line satisfies both equations.

steps.title: Parametrize the solution set using y = r
steps.content:
For any real number $r$, set $y = r$. Substitute into $2x + 3y = 7$:
$$2x + 3r = 7$$
$$x = -\frac{3r}{2} + \frac{7}{2}$$
All solutions are of the form $\left(-\frac{3r}{2} + \frac{7}{2}, r\right)$. This matches choice B exactly — B is correct.
